options: An installable and executable Android app package
An Android App Bundle used as a publishing format

que: Can users directly install an `.aab` file on Android devices like an APK?
options: No, app bundles are for publishing and must be processed into APKs

que: What does Google Play do with an uploaded app bundle?
options: Converts it into optimized APKs for device configurations
Turns it into a Room database
Runs it directly on user devices

que: Why can AAB improve user downloads?
options: Because users download only resources and code relevant to their device
Because it forces one universal package for all devices

que: Which statement about AAB is correct according to Android Developers?
options: It is proprietary and only works conceptually on Play
It is open source and can be supported by other stores

que: Which file format do developers usually upload to Google Play now for new apps?
Which statement best summarizes the relationship between APK and AAB?
options: They are identical names for the same artifact
AAB is the publishing input and APK is the installable output
APK is only for tablets and AAB is only for phones
AAB is for debug and APK is for release

que: What is a common misunderstanding about app bundles?
options: That Play generates optimized APKs from them
That they organize code and resources into modules
That they are directly installable like APKs
That they are used for publishing

que: Which official docs best support this topic?
options: Android App Bundle overview, format docs, and FAQ
